title: cephalocereus tehuacanus
intro: Cephalocereus tehuacanus, also known as the Tejuino cactus, is a species of cactus native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its cylindrical stems and short, fine spines. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cephalocereus tehuacanus include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing relief from pain and itching. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by boiling or infusing its stems in water, and it is often used in traditional medicine to create teas, tinctures, and ointments.
